title: REST API overrides container entrypoint to empty string when not provided to the create endpoint
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Docker compatible API does not retain an images entrypoint if one is defined when creating a container. The entrypoint in the created container is set to an empty string instead of the entrypoint of the image as expected.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Build a trivial container with an `ENTRYPOINT` line

2. Create a container using `podman create`

3. Create a similar container using the docker compat API

**Describe the results you received:**

In the container created as a result of the API POST, the entrypoint is set to an empty string.

**Describe the results you expected:**

The container created as a result of the API POST should properly inherit its entrypoint from the image when not overridden in the POSTed data.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Shell transcript  of the repro:

```
sh-5.0$ podman build - <<EOF
FROM alpine
ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/echo"]
EOF
STEP 1: FROM alpine
STEP 2: ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/echo"]
STEP 3: COMMIT
--> 71a571c4423
71a571c4423108222a3c4b25e1c9633e4585954845251329740a7a07370990c6
sh-5.0$ podman inspect 71a571c4423 | jq '.[0]["Config"]'
{
  "Env": [
    "PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin"
  ],
  "Entrypoint": [
    "/bin/echo"
  ]
}
sh-5.0$ podman create 71a571c4423 foo
fc3b821c480f717657551579f0e46876c9926cd9cfa11841c18a36243c6f72a4
sh-5.0$ podman inspect fc3b821c480f | jq '.[0]["Config"]'

}
sh-5.0$ podman start fc3b821c480f
fc3b821c480f717657551579f0e46876c9926cd9cfa11841c18a36243c6f72a4

{"Id":"ac1107fe17eb33888ad58c8a5256e9c65f371c3c537be149caf3c3100b7e2155","Warnings":[]}

Error: unable to start container "ac1107fe17eb": executable file not found in $PATH: No such file or directory: OCI runtime command not found error
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical host.

comments.body: I suspect that https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/pkg/spec/createconfig.go#L293 might be to blame here. I think that gets set by https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/pkg/api/handlers/compat/containers_create.go#L179, presumably to `nil` since I didn't provide an entrypoint in the JSON. The comment at https://github.com/containers/libpod/tree/master/libpod/options.go#L1189 suggests that passing in `nil` to `WithEntrypoint` isn't what we want to do in this case.

It's also a little sad that the new API doesn't yet return the entrypoint when I inspect the image or containers either, foiling my attempts to work around the bug :( That's TODO'd at https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/pkg/api/handlers/types.go#L250

@rhatdan - just saw #6597, thanks for getting that in so quickly! I'm not sure how your notation for `Fixes` works here but just to be clear, that change will help me work around the bug I reported here but not actually fix it.
Fixes will close this Issue when it merges, if it does not fix the entire issue, then you can reopen it.
